commands.review_summary:
The branch raises macOS chat.send run timeout to five minutes while keeping the WebSocket request wait at 30 seconds, wires macOS completion waiting through agent.wait, increases the shared chat pending-run timeout, and adds Swift regression tests.

PR surface: Other +180. Total +180 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. live, high-confidence reproduction was run. Source inspection on current main shows the macOS chat.send default is 30 seconds and shared pending timeout is 120 seconds, matching the reported premature-timeout path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for real macOS behavior proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply from this review.

target.title: Hatching assistant hangs after purpose when using OpenAI 5.5
target.body:
Related: problem-mode user report; no public issue number

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes macOS onboarding/web chat using a short request deadline as the actual assistant run budget. With slower first replies, such as a newly configured OpenAI 5.5 session, the macOS hatch/chat path could appear stuck or time out before the assistant run should have been considered done.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Split the macOS `chat.send` run budget from the WebSocket ACK/request wait.
- Keep the ACK wait short at 30 seconds so an unreachable gateway still fails promptly.
- Send a five-minute `timeoutMs` for the assistant run and use the same five-minute budget for shared chat UI pending-run completion.
- Did not change gateway protocol shape, provider routing, auth, model catalog behavior, session storage, or delivery channels.

## User Impact

macOS users hatching or chatting with a newly configured assistant get the same first-run-sized wait window used by terminal hatch. A slow first reply should remain pending instead of being treated as stuck or timed out after the old shorter macOS/shared chat budgets.

## Evidence

- Branch base checked during freshness gate: `main@ff1e7e1305cc63f6a5d9aba73426cabf4861a24f`. No final rebase was performed.
- Real behavior proof:
  - **Behavior or issue addressed:** macOS `chat.send` and shared chat UI pending-run timeout were shorter than the first-run hatch budget, making slow first replies look stuck or prematurely timed out.
  - **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.5.1 arm64, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2, Swift 6.3.2 from Command Line Tools, OpenClaw worktree SHA `2603cd0`.
  - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
    ```bash
    swiftformat --lint apps/macos/Sources --config config/swiftformat --exclude '**/OpenClawProtocol,**/HostEnvSecurityPolicy.generated.swift'
    git diff --check
    TOOLCHAIN_DIR="/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools" swiftlint lint --config config/swiftlint.yml
    swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter "GatewayConnectionControlTests"
    swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter "ChatViewModelTests"
    ```
  - **Evidence after fix:**
    ```text
    SwiftFormat:
    Running SwiftFormat...
    (lint mode - no files will be changed.)
    SwiftFormat completed in 0.06s.
    0/256 files require formatting, 2 files skipped.

    git diff --check:
    passed with no output

    SwiftLint with TOOLCHAIN_DIR=/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools:
    Done linting! Found 11 violations, 0 serious in 256 files.
    Existing warnings remain warnings; the changed format failure is gone.

    SwiftPM focused tests on this machine:
    swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter "GatewayConnectionControlTests"
    swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter "ChatViewModelTests"
    both compile until OpenClawChatUI previews and then stop because this host has only Command Line Tools, not full Xcode:
    external macro implementation type 'PreviewsMacros.SwiftUIView' could not be found for macro 'Preview(_:body:)'; plugin for module 'PreviewsMacros' not found

    Repository macOS 26 CI after push at 2603cd0:
    macos-swift -> pass in 3m47s
    macos-node -> pass in 41s
    Real behavior proof -> pass in 12s
    ios-build -> pass in 1m25s
    android-build-play -> pass in 1m41s
    android-test-play -> pass in 31s
    android-test-third-party -> pass in 1m49s
    ```
  - **Observed result after fix:** The macOS source format gate no longer reports the `agentWait` redundant variable failure. Source-level behavior remains the intended split: `chat.send.timeoutMs` defaults to 300000 ms for the run budget, the WebSocket request wait remains 30000 ms, and shared chat pending completion uses 300000 ms.
  - **What was not tested:** Full local Xcode build/test and visual macOS app proof are not complete on this host because full Xcode is not installed. I attempted both Mac App Store and `xcodes` installation; the App Store path required sudo in a non-interactive session, and `xcodes` required Apple ID credentials. The pushed branch should use the repository macOS 26 runner for the full native lane.
- Regression tests and local checks:
  ```text
  swiftformat --lint apps/macos/Sources --config config/swiftformat --exclude '**/OpenClawProtocol,**/HostEnvSecurityPolicy.generated.swift' -> pass, 0/256 files require formatting
  git diff --check -> pass
  TOOLCHAIN_DIR=/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools swiftlint lint --config config/swiftlint.yml -> completed with 11 warnings, 0 serious
  swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter GatewayConnectionControlTests -> blocked locally by missing full-Xcode Preview macro plugin
  swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests -> blocked locally by missing full-Xcode Preview macro plugin
  GitHub macos-swift at 2603cd0 -> pass, 3m47s
  GitHub Real behavior proof at 2603cd0 -> pass, 12s
  GitHub macos-node at 2603cd0 -> pass, 41s
  ```

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Swift regression tests plus macOS CI lane.
- Target test file: `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/GatewayConnectionControlTests.swift`; `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`.
- Scenario locked in: default macOS chat send carries a 300000 ms run timeout, `agent.wait` receives the same timeout for completion, and shared chat completion wait uses 300000 ms.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the assertions sit at the two affected client boundaries: outgoing macOS gateway payloads and shared chat UI completion waiting.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: macOS chat used one 30-second value for two lifecycle layers: the quick WebSocket request wait and the actual assistant run budget. Shared chat UI pending state also used a shorter 120-second timeout.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression asserted the macOS `chat.send.timeoutMs` payload or shared chat completion wait against the five-minute first-run hatch budget.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
